explanation: The Sigma rule detects base64 encoded commands using '-encodedcommand' or '-e'. The command is modified using substitution by replacing '-encodedcommand' with its short form '-enc', which is functionally equivalent and still matches the rule.

The '-encodedcommand' flag is further shortened to '-e', which is a valid and equivalent syntax for passing base64 encoded commands in PowerShell. This is a safe substitution and still triggers the Sigma rule.
Quotes are inserted around the encoded command to test how the rule handles string parsing. This is a safe insertion technique and does not alter the behavior of the command.
A newline character is appended to the command line using an escape character. This tests how the rule handles whitespace and escape characters in the command line.
Extra whitespace is added at the end of the command line. This is a safe mutation technique to test how the rule handles trailing spaces in the command line.
